def: s.f. Perfection; completion; conclusion, end, termination; totality, the whole; brocade, cloth of gold, tissue.
s.m. corr. of تنبان tambān, q.v.
s.m. = tamāćā, q.v. s.m. Slap, box, blow
s.m. A pistol ( = tabanća)
subs: tamanća dāg̠nā, v.n. To fire or discharge a pistol
v.n. To pull or draw out a pistol.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمنچه (tamanča), from Ottoman Turkish طبانجه (tabanca, “pistol”).

def: s.m. A pistol ( = tabanća, q.v.). s.m. = tamanćā, q.v. s.m. Slap, box, blow

ety: for تمانچه tamānća, or تپانچه tapānća
def: s.m. A slap (on the face), a box, thump; — a pistol
subs: or t̤amānća ṣaḥīḥ karnā, or t̤amānća lagānā, or t̤amānća mārnā (-ko), To give (one) a slap, &c.
(apnā), 'To keep (one's) face red by slapping'; (met.) to conceal (one's) poverty from pride or high spirit.

ety: corr. of A. طمانينة t̤umānīnat, inf. n. of اِطمانّ 'to be still,' &c.; rt. طمن
def: s.f. Rest, repose, tranquillity, calm, composure.

ety: for A. tamannī, inf. n. vi of مني 'to try, to afflict,' &c.
def: s.f. Wish, desire, longing, inclination ( = ārzū); reqnest, prayer, supplication, petition
subs: (-kī), To wish (for), to desire; to pray or petition (for), &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَمَنَّا (tamannā), from Arabic تَمَنَّى (tamannā).

def: s.f. See tamannā.
s.m. A red-coloured lotus; copper; gold.
s.f. A pond full of lotus-flowers.

ety: inf. n. v of مرد 'to be very wicked, or insolent,' &c.
def: s.m. Being refractory, or stubborn, &c.; refractoriness, stubbornness, obstinacy; disobedience, insolence, contumacy, rebellion; (in law) contempt, resistance (to orders or proceedings).
s.f. = tamarrud.

ety: inf. n. vi of مرض 'to fall sick'
def: s.m. Feigning a disease

adj. & s.m. Dark; affected by or appertaining to the third quality or that of darkness (vice, ignorance, &c.); inert; stupid, ignorant; vicious, vindictive; irascible; malignant; — a malignant or mischievous person, a villain, &c.
s.m. Darkness, ignorance, illusion, error; anger, irascibility, &c. (cf. next).

s.m. Darkness (physical or moral), gloom; the quality of darkness incident to humanity; illusion of mind; head-strong passion, anger, irascibility, vindictiveness; sorrow, grief; the gloom of hell; name of one of the hells; a name of Rāhū or the personified ascending node
subs: s.m. 'Night-walker,' an epithet of any rākshas
s.m. The third of the qualities incident to creation or the state of humanity, viz., the quality of darkness or ignorance ( = tamas, q.v.)

adj. & s.m. Having the quality of tamas predominant in the temperament, irascible, ignorant, proud, &c.; — one who is under the influence of tamas, a mischievous or malignant person, incendiary, villain, &c.
adj. & s.m. Destroying darkness; — the destroyer of darkness; the sun; the moon; fire; an epithet of the legislator Buddh
adj. Obscured, clouded; overcome with or influenced by rage, fear, &c., or any of the effects of the property of tamas or darkness.

ety: for A. tamāshī, inf. n. vi of مشي 'to walk'
def: s.m. Walking abroad for recreation; entertainment, exhibition, show, sight, spectacle; sport, amusement, pleasure, fun, jest, joke; anything strange or curious
subs: (or, more com.) tamāsh-bīn, s.m. A sight-seer; a libertine, rake; an epicure (syn. aubāsh)
tamāsh-bīnī, s.f. Luxury; libertinism, licentiousness, gallantry, raking, whoring
(-ko), To show (one) a sight or spectacle, &c.; (fig.) to give (one) a trouncing
v.n. To see a sight or spectacle, to see what is to be seen; to look on at fun or sport, &c.
v.n. To see; to take a walk; to make sport or fun; to exhibit, play, act a part; to poke fun (at), make fun (of), to jeer, jest
s.m. One who makes sport; a showman; player, actor
adj. Sight-seeing, beholding, looking at for recreation or amusement
A strange, curious, or funny thing; a pretty jest
tamashā-gāh, s.f. A spectacle, show; a place of show, theatre, circus, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تماشا (tamāšā), ultimately from Arabic مَشَى (mašā, “to walk”). Compare Bengali তামাশা (tamaśa).

def: s.m.f. Fit for a show or spectacle; — a spectator, looker-on; libertine, rake; epicure.
wety: From تماشا (tamāśā) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. = tāmas, q.v. adj. & s.m. Dark; affected by or appertaining to the third quality or that of darkness (vice, ignorance, &c.); inert; stupid, ignorant; vicious, vindictive; irascible; malignant; — a malignant or mischievous person, a villain, &c.
s.m. Jesting, joking, buffoonery.

ety: inf. n. vi of مسّ 'to touch'
def: s.m. Touching one another; contact.

ety: inf. n. v of مسك 'to hold fast'
def: s.m. An obligation, bond, promissory note, note of hand, writing, instrument, receipt.
s.m. pl. (of tamassuk), Obligations, bonds, &c.
adj. Pertaining to a bond or note of hand.

ety: inf. n. v of مثل 'to be like'
def: s.m. Resembling, imitating; applying or employing an allegory, parable, fable, or proverb.

ety: inf. n. vi of مسخ 'to change,' &c.
def: Metamorphosing, transforming; transmigration (of souls); effacing. (The word is not classical Arabic.)

ety: inf. n. vi of مثل 'to be like'
def: s.m. Being like; being equal; equal part or division (of an estate, &c.).
s.f. Night, dark night.
adj. Desirous, cupidinous, longing or hankering (after).
(contrac. fr. A. t̤āmmāt, pl. of t̤ammat, 'a calamity.' rt. طمّ), s.m. pl. Inconsistent words or speech; unintelligible or foolish speech; words barbarously uttered; raving; vain-glorying, boasting.

ety: inf. n. v of متع 'to enjoy'
def: s.m. Using enjoying, reaping advantage, gaining; enjoyment, delight, pleasure.

ety: inf. n. v of موج 'to roll in billows'
def: s.m. Agitation (of waves), billowing or fluctuation (of water).

ety: inf. n. v of مول 'to be rich'
def: s.m. The being or becoming rich; riches, wealth.
s.m. = tām, and tāmā (see tāṅbā, the more correct form).
s.m. = tām, and tāmā (see tāṅbā, the more correct form).
s.m. corr. of تنبان tambān, q.v.
s.m. corr. of tambān, q.v.
s.m. See تنباکو tambākū.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَنْبَاکُو (tanbākū).

def: s.m. Tobacco (in P. also tambākū; A. & T. tumbāk
subs: Some derive the Hindī word from the S. tāmra-kuṭṭaka
v.n. To smoke tobacco, to have a smoke.

def: s.m. See تنبالو tambālū.
s.m. A copper or brass pot or bowl; cooking-pot; pot, pipkin (syn. tambiyā; baṭ-lohī).
s.m. = تنبان tambān, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. A. t̤unub, corr. to t̤anab
def: s.f. A pavilion, an open tent (cf. tambū); — an open-fronted summer room.

ety: inf. n. ii of نبه 'to recollect,' &c.
def: s.f. Admonition, reproof, censure, reprimand; correction, punishment
subs: tambīh karnā, v.t. To admonish; to reprimand, &c.

def: s.m. See تنبيا tambiyā.
s.m. A copper or brass pot or bowl (syn. tambālū; baṭ-lohī (dialectic forms of the word are tamheṛī, taṅbheṛī, taṅbeṛī).
v.n. To become coppery; to become covered with verdigris; to acquire or have a coppery taste (food).

ety: Prk. तंबोलं; S. ताम्बूलं; P. tambūl
def: s.m. The betel leaf (syn. pān)
subs: v.n. To bleed at the mouth (a horse, through injury from the bridle).

def: = H تمبولن तम्बोलिन tambolin = H تمبولي तम्बोली tambolī See تنبول tambol, &c
s.m. A caste whose business it is to sell betel-leaf; a member of that caste; a seller of betel-leaf.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀁𑀩𑁄𑀮𑀺𑀅 (taṃbolia), from Sanskrit ताम्बूलिक (tāmbūlika).

def: = H تمبول तम्बोल tambol = H تمبولن तम्बोलिन tambolin See تنبول tambol, &c
= H تمبول तम्बोल tambol = H تمبولي तम्बोली tambolī See تنبول tambol, &c
s.f. A woman of the tambolī caste; a woman whose business it is to sell betel-leaf; the wife of a seller of betel-leaf; — name of a tree.

s.m. = tāṅbṛā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Copper-coloured, dark red (e.g. tāṅbṛī maṭṭī, 'red earth'); — copper-colour (in pigeons, &c.); a copper-coloured gem of inferior value; a false stone resembling a ruby; — a bald head.
s.m. See تنبو tambū.
s.m. A tent (particularly a large one); pavilion; canopy; — an eel, a lamprey
s.m. The leaf of the Piper betel ( = pān); — the areca nut
subs: s.m. A kind of pulse, Ervum lens
s.f. The Piper betel (bearing a pungent leaf).

def: s.f. Betel-leaf ( = tāmbūl).
s.m. The Pān-bearer, a servant who prepares and presents the pān; a seller of betel ( = tambolī, q.v.).
s.m. A Turkish guitar (with six wires or strings), a mandoline; (in India) a tabor, drum.

ety: S. तुम्ब+र+कः; whence H. tūṅbṛā or tumbṛā, 'a gourd'; A. t̤umbūr or t̤ambūr, which is fancifully derived from the P. dumb-i-bara, 'a lamb's tail'
def: s.m. A kind of mandoline, or Turkish guitar, with chords of brass wire; a tambour; a drum.
s.m. See تنبوره tambūra.
s.m. A small kind of t̤ambūr; — also = t̤ambūr.

ety: S. तुम्ब+र+कः; whence H. tūṅbṛā or tumbṛā, 'a gourd'; A. t̤umbūr or t̤ambūr, which is fancifully derived from the P. dumb-i-bara, 'a lamb's tail'
def: s.m. A kind of mandoline, or Turkish guitar, with chords of brass wire; a tambour; a drum.
s.m. corr. of tambūra, q.v.
s.m. A player on the t̤ambūr.
s.m. A drummer; one who plays the tabor.
adj. Coveting, covetous, very desircus, greedy.
s.m. Calx or scoriæ of copper.
s.m. Calx or scoriæ of copper.

ety: in T. also damg̠ā, by invers. from dag̠mā; cf. dāg̠ & S. dagdha
def: s.m. Stamp (on gold, silver, &c.), seal; brand or mark (on an animal); trade-mark; device on a shield; arms or armorial bearings; a medal; a royal grant or charter, a diploma; — a tax on travellers. (The vulg. and, originally, correct form of the word is tag̠ma or tag̠mā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمغا (tamġā), from Chagatai تمغا (tamğā).

ety: inf. n. ii of مہد 'to spread equally'
def: s.f. Arranging, disposing; arrangement, disposition, adjustment, settlement, management; confirmation; preliminary, preamble, introduction, preface, preparative; pleading an excuse
subs: v.n. To take up, or to open (a subject); to premise
(-kī), To make an arrangement or disposition (of), to dispose, &c.; to premise
v.n. To lay down premises; to frame vain suppositions beforehand, to make idle and shuffling excuses; — tamhīdeṅ bāṅdhā karnā, To go on premising; to continue making vain suppositions, &c.

def: s.m. Dispositions; preparations, preliminary measures or arrangements, &c.
adj. Preliminary, introductory.
s.f. Night, darkness.

ety: inf. n. ii of عمد 'to prop or support'
def: s.f. Propping up; damming (a stream, &c.); — baptizing.

ety: inf. n. ii of عمل 'to do, act, perform,' &c.
def: s.f. Causing to act, putting in force (a decree, &c.), carrying out (an order, &c.), executing; execution (of an order), performance; exercise (of)
subs: (-kī). To carry out, or to execute (an order, &c.), to put in esecution.

ety: inf. n. ii of عمّ 'to be common or general'
def: s.f. Rendering universal; generality, universality; indefiniteness.

ety: inf. n. ii of عمر 'to make habitable; to build,' &c.
def: s.f. Building, constructing; construction, structure; rebuilding; repairing
s.m. The dark half of the month from full moon to new moon; — anger, wrath, indignation; — a division of hell, that of deep gloom.
s.f. A dark night, or one during the wane of the moon.
s.m. The dark half of the month, from the full to the change.

ety: for A. تعمية taʻmiyat, fem., inf. n. ii of عمي 'to be blind'
def: s.m. Rendering blind; making the meaning (of a verse, or of speech, &c.) obscure, covert, or enigmatical; an enigma.

ety: for A. تمييز tamyīz, inf. n. ii of ميز 'to separate,' &c.
def: s.f. Discernment, judgment, discrimination, distinction, discretion, sense
subs: adj. Possessing discernment, discerning, judicious, discreet, sensible; well-conducted, well-mannered
(-kī?), To distinguish, to discriminate (between); to discern, perceive
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمیز (tamīz), itself from Arabic تَمْيِيز (tamyīz).

def: s.m. A rough kind of sedan chair carried by two men, a 'tonjon' (see thām-jān).

ety: inf. n. ii of مجد 'to excel in glory'
def: s.f. Glorifying (God), glorification (of God); the hymn or prayer that is pronounced from the minarets of a mosque an hour and a half before dawn.
s.m. Heat; sun-stroke.
s.f. Gravity, dignity, majesty, grandeur, greatness, authority, power.
s.f. A small timbrel or drum (made of iron, wood, or clay); a small circular plate of brass or mixed metal, a gong.
s.f. Gravity, dignity, majesty, grandeur, greatness, authority, power.
s.m. Tumbler, drinking glass.

ety: inf. n. ii of ملك 'to possess'
def: s.f. Appointing master or possessor; possessorship; possession

ety: part. n. of تمّ 'to become complete'
def: adj. Complete, entire, whole, full; perfect, consummate.
adj. Very covetous, avaricious, wishful, extremely greedy.
(3rd. pers. sing. perf. of تمّ), It is completed, is finished, is complete
ex: کیا تم مجھ سے محبت کرتی ہو؟
kyā tum mujh-se mohabbat kartī ho?
تم جیسے لوگوں کی ہی ضرورت ہے یہاں۔
tum jaise logõ kī hī zarūrat hai yahā̃.
People like you are needed here.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुम्हि (tumhi), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe), from Sanskrit युष्मान् (yuṣmān, acc. pl.) adopting the initial sounds of त्वम् (tvám, nom. sg.).

def: (3rd pers. sing. fem. of the perf. of تمّ); See tamma.

s.m. See s.v. tamas for compounds of which the first member is tamo. adj. & s.m. Dark; affected by or appertaining to the third quality or that of darkness (vice, ignorance, &c.); inert; stupid, ignorant; vicious, vindictive; irascible; malignant; — a malignant or mischievous person, a villain, &c.
s.m. A ripe date, a date
adj. & s.m. Of a coppery red colour; — coppery, consisting of copper; — copper; a dark or coppery red; — a kind of sandal-wood (said to be of a dark red colour and to smell like the lotus)
subs: s.m. A copper plate (on which Hindū grants of land, &c. are frequently inscribed)
s.m. A copper-coloured or red flower; the plant Bauhinia variegata, or a kind of ebony; — the plant Kæmpferia rotunda
s.m. A red kind of sandal, Pterospermum santolinum
s.f. Name of the female elephant of the western quarter
s.m. Sulphate of copper, blue vitriol
s.m. A red sort of sandal wood; the plant Dolichos uniflorus
s.t. The China rose; the blossom of sesamum
s.f. The plant Rubia mungista, Bengal madder
s.m. A kind of vetch, Dolichos uniflorus.

def: s.m. = tāṅbṛā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Copper-coloured, dark red (e.g. tāṅbṛī maṭṭī, 'red earth'); — copper-colour (in pigeons, &c.); a copper-coloured gem of inferior value; a false stone resembling a ruby; — a bald head.
s.m. Name of one of the eighteen divisions of the known continent.

s.f. A copper or metallic cup of prescribed capacity and perforated by a small hole at the bottom (when placed in a vessel of water it answers the purpose of a clepsydra, and the water gradually filling it marks the time that has elapsed).
adj. & s.m. Coppery, made of copper; — a coppersmith, brazier.

s.f. A coppersmith's wife; — a kind of clepsydra ( = tāmrī, q.v.). s.f. A copper or metallic cup of prescribed capacity and perforated by a small hole at the bottom (when placed in a vessel of water it answers the purpose of a clepsydra, and the water gradually filling it marks the time that has elapsed).

s.f. Likening, comparing; assimilation; comparison, similitude, resemblance, fac-simile, counterpart; example, allegory, fable, apologue; — tams̤īl lānā, v.n. To adduce examples; to employ parables, &c.; to allegorize.
adv. By way of similitude, allegorically, as an example.
s.f. pl. (of tams̤īl), Examples, allegories, &c.
s.f. A small timbrel or drum, a tom-tom; the sound of a drum or tom-tom.

ety: fr. tamtamānā; — ˚āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+त्वं
def: s.f. The state of growing red (in the face); flush, glow.

ety: tamtam˚ = S. तम् redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.n. To become or grow red (in the face), to flush; to glow; to sparkle; to twinkle; to flash.
s.m. The Syrian month corresponding to July O.S.

s.f. A tone; a tune; the key-note (in music); — stretching, tension; knitting (the eyebrows); — a thread; a stretched or tight cord or rope; a rigid rod or bar, a tie-rod (as the rods which give fixity to the poles of a pālkī, or the iron bar of a cart, or across a bedstead)
subs: tān toṛnā, v.n. To beat time; to strike up a tune; to strike the key-note; — to crack a joke; to drop a word which induces conversation or excites a quarrel; to animadvert
v.n. To strike up a tune; to sing or play in tune; — to poke fun (at), to crack a joke ( = ṭhaṭṭhā mārnā)
v.n. To pitch or to catch the key-note; to tune.

def: s.f. The plant Verbena (or Zepania) nodiflora.
s.m. The body, person; one's own person, self
subs: s.f. Ease of body, bodily comfort, indulgence
adj. & s.m. Careful of the body, self-indulgent; selfish; luxurious, voluptuous; — a self-indulgent person, &c.
s.f. A pampering of the body, self-indulgence, ease, luxury, voluptuousness
adj. Well in health, sound in body, healthy, vigorous
v.t. To make (one) well, to restore to health, to cure
adj. & s.m. Applying oneself (to), attentive (to), of great application, diligent; — one who has great application, &c.
s.f. Application, attention, diligence; exertion, effort, pains
adv. Attentively, diligently; energetically, with exertion or effort, with painstaking
tan denā (-meṅ), To give or apply oneself (to), to pay attention (to), to exert oneself (in), to take pains
s.f. A cloth of the nainsukh kind, but finer; a kind of vest worn under the qabā
adj. & s.m. Taking ease, reposing, indulging; idling; bodily ease, &c. = tan-āsānī, q.v.
(-ke), To touch or affect the person (of); to come home to one, to feel; to assimilate (food), to nourish, to make flesh, to fatten; to be applied to the body, be brought into use
adj. Personally attached (to, -ke), devoted (to), zealous (for); active, energetic, painstaking
s.m. Body and soul, one's whole self
adv. With body and soul, with all (one's) heart and soul

tan-o-man mārnā, v.n. To restrain the appetites or desires; to suppress the feelings or emotions; to keep quiet or silent; to concentrate one's attention or faculties (on), to render oneself unconscious of snrrounding objects, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀡𑀽 (taṇū), from Sanskrit तनू (tanū́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tanúHs, from Proto-Indo-European *tenúh₂s, from *tenuh₂- (“body”).

def: s.m. Twang; sound (com. ṭhan)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀡𑀽 (taṇū), from Sanskrit तनू (tanū́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tanúHs, from Proto-Indo-European *tenúh₂s, from *tenuh₂- (“body”).

def: s.m. Haughtiness, pride, conceit, vanity.

ety: inf. n. of طعن 'to pierce or wound with a spear,' &c.
def: s.m. Thrusting or striking with a spear; a thrust, a stab; — blame, reproach, disapprobation, &c. ( = t̤aʻna, q.v.).
adv., corr. of tahāṅ, q.v.
s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: v.t. To heat; to raise heat (by blowing with bellows, &c.); to anneal, to temper; to melt, fuse; to twist (by heating = tāʼo denā); to prove, test, assay.
s.m. = tānā, q.v. s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: s.m. Stalk, stock, trunk, stem; — a spider's web.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنه.

ety: for A. طعنة, n. of un. fr. t̤aʻn, q.v.
def: s.m. Blame, reproach, chiding, taunting, taunt, reviling, ridicule, scoff, gibe, derision; ignominy, disgrace; aspersion
subs: (corr. of t̤aʻna-tashnīʻ), s.m. pl. Taunts, scoffs, gibes, sneers, &c.
or t̤aʻne denā (-ko), To taunt, to ridicule, deride, &c.
s.m. A reproacher, reviler, taunter, asperser, scoffer, &c.
(-par), To jeer (at), scoff (at), to ridicule, deride, taunt, &c. ( = t̤aʻna denā)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَعْنَه (ta'na), borrowed from Arabic طَعْنَة (ṭaʕna), from طَعَنَ (ṭaʕana). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi طعنہ (t̤'nh /⁠ta'na⁠/).

def: s.m. corr. of طعنه taʻna, q.v. s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: s.m. Pudendum muliebre; clitoris.

ety: inf. n. v of نعم 'to be in a state of ease,' &c.
def: s.m. Enjoying in abundance the conveniences and comforts of life; enjoyment, ease, happiness; prosperity.

ety: prob. corr. of A. اطناب at̤nāb, pl. of t̤unub, 'a tent rope'; rt. طنب
def: s.f. A tent-rope; a long rope used in measuring land.

ety: inf. n. v. of نبه 'to recollect,' &c.
def: s.m. f. (?), Advice, admonition; animadversion, &c. (see tambīh); — bashfulness, modesty.

ety: inf. n. vi of ند 'to flee'
def: s.m. Being dispersed, flying in disorder; dispersion, flight.

ety: inf. n. v of ندم 'to repent'
def: s.m. Repentance, penitence; shame, bashfulness, modesty.

ety: inf. n. v of نفر 'to run away in fright'
def: s.m. Avoiding, shunning; aversion, disgust.

ety: inf. n. v of نفس; from nafas 'breath'
def: s.m. Breathing, respiration; heaving a deep sigh.

ety: inf. n. vi of نفي 'to prohibit, to deny'
def: s.f. Expelling; pursuing, persecuting, seeking to ruin one another; denial (that another has fulfilled a contract or paid a debt, &c.).

ety: inf. n. vi of نفر 'to run away in fright'
def: s.m. Flying from one another; mutual repugnance or aversion; disputing or contending (for honour) before a judge.

ety: inf. n. vi of نہي 'to reach, arrive'
def: s.f. Reaching to the end, arriving (at the end of a journey, &c.); being brought to a termination; finish, completion.
s.f. A harsh sound; jingle, tinkle, ring (of metal, china, &c.); throb, beat, shooting pain; spirit, mettle.

ety: inf. n. ii of تخنخ 'to remove,' &c.
def: s.m. Clearing the throat, hemming, hawking.

ety: inf. n. vi of نقل 'to transport,' &c.
def: s.m. A communicating together; mutual communication.

ety: inf. n. vi of نقض 'to break; to violate'
def: s.m. Being discordant or incompatible; contradiction; discrepancy; incompatibility; (in law) the advancing of two incompatible claims (to the same property).
v.t. To extend, stretch, pull, tighten ( = tānnā).

ety: S. तरूण, or तनु+Prk. प्पअं = त्पअं = त्वअं = S. त्व+कं
def: s.m. Youth; buoyancy or elasticity of youth; pride of youth.

ety: inf. n. v of نسل 'to bring forth'
def: s.m. Pedigree, genealogy. (The form is not met with in classical Arabic).

ety: inf. n. v of نصر; derived from naṣrān, 'a Christian'
def: s.m. Becoming a Christian (or Nazarene).

ety: inf. n. vi of نسب 'to mention the lineage' (of)
def: s.m. Resemblance; proportionateness, proportion; relation.
s.m. pl. (of tanāsub), Proportions, relations.

ety: inf. n. vi of نسخ 'to transform'
def: s.m. Transformation, transmigration, metempsychosis.

ety: inf. n. vi of نسل 'to beget, bring forth'
def: s.m. Begetting; generation; uninterrupted descent through a series of generations
adj. See s.v. tan.

ety: inf. n. vi of نول 'to give, present'
def: s.m. Taking meat and drink, eating
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَنَاوُل (tanāwul), from Arabic تَنَاوُل (tanāwul).

def: s.m. A son; a male descendant.
s.f. A daughter; a female descendant
adj. Disputed, at issue, moot; — s.m. = tanāzǒʻ.

ety: inf. n. vi. of تزع 'to remove, to pull up,' &c.
def: s.m. Disputing, wrangling; dispute, contention, strife.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَنَازُع (tanāzuʕ).

ety: inf. n. vi of نزل 'to descend'
def: s.m. Alighting; descent; decline, fall (of price, &c.), loss (of rank, &c.); leaping from (a horse, in order to fight), coming or rushing down into a plain against each other (two armies).

ety: inf. n. v of نزه 'to restrain oneself'
def: s.m. Being pure, keeping oneself free from vice or stain; studying continence; being modest or chaste.

ety: inf. n. v of نزل 'to alight'
def: s.m. Descent, decline, diminution, wane, decay, falling off; degradation
subs: v.n. To fall off, decline; to be degraded, &c.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Copper
subs: s.m. A copper rupee, a bad rupee
s.m. Sulphate of copper, blue vitriol.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀁𑀩 (taṃba), from Sanskrit ताम्र (tāmrá).

def: s.m. The food of hawks and other birds of chase.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀁𑀩 (taṃba), from Sanskrit ताम्र (tāmrá).

def: adj. & s.m. Copper-coloured, dark red (e.g. tāṅbṛī maṭṭī, 'red earth'); — copper-colour (in pigeons, &c.); a copper-coloured gem of inferior value; a false stone resembling a ruby; — a bald head.
adj. & s.m. Cross-grained, perverse, &c. = ṭāṅćṛā, q.v.; — moving crookedly or tortuously; moving round in a circle, hovering (a bird); — perversity, perverse tricks; troublesomeness, annoyance
subs: ṭāṅć lānā (-se), To practise perverse or annoying tricks (with), to annoy, to endeavour to get up a quarrel (with).

def: adj. Miserly, hard, untractable.

adj. = tang, q.v. adj. Contracted, straitened, confined, strait, narrow, tight; wanting, scarce, scanty, stinted, barren; distressed, poor, badly off; distracted, troubled, vexed; dejected, sad, sick (at heart); — s.m. A horse-belt, girth; — a bag, sack; half a horse's, bullock's, or camel's load; — a bell
subs: or ba-tang ānā (-se), To be distressed or incommoded (by), to be in distress or difficulty; to be troubled, or vexed, or harassed (by); to be utterly weary or sick (of), to have one's patience exhausted (by); to be dejected or sad
adj. Wearing tight clothes, tightly dressed
adj. Straitened in circumstances, poor, distressed, destitute, in great straits
s.f. Narrow circumstances, straits, distress, poverty, adversity
adj. 'Of narrow stomach or capacity'; unable to keep a secret ( = tunuk-z̤arf)
adj. Poor, penniless; close-fisted, miserly, niggard
s.f. Poverty, penury, want; inability (to do any thing), helplessness; — parsimony, niggardliness
adj. & s.m. Narrow-hearted, illiberal, miserly, niggard ( = tang-ćashm); sick at heart, dejected, sad, sorrowful ( = dil-tang); — a miser, a niggard
s.f. Niggardliness, stinginess, parsimony; — heart-ache, grief, sorrow, dejection ( = dil-tangī)
adj. Small-mouthed, an epithet of a sweetheart
v.t. To contract, straiten, narrow, tighten; to distress, vex, worry, harass; to put pressure upon, to compel, to oppress
tang khaiṅćnā, v.n. To tighten the girth (of a saddle)
(-kā), To contract, take in, tighten (a knot, buckle, &c.)
s.m. tang-nāʼe, s.f. A narrow place or passage, a strait; a defile
s.m. Moment of difficulty, critical moment, emergency; hard times
v.n. To be in straits, be badly off, be penniless
or ba tang honā, v.n. = tang ānā, q.v.

def: v.n. To move crookedly or tortuously; to fly round in a circle, to hover (as a bird); to practise tortuosity, or deceit, or wheedling, &c. (with), to cajole, wheedle, inveigle.

ety: ṭāṅć, q.v. +ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: adj. Cross-grained, perverse, froward, troublesome; moving tortuously to obtain an end; audacious.

s.f. (m.?), A scaffolding (in a field of corn, &c. on which a person keeps watch when the grain is ripe to frighten away birds and other animals), a raised seat, stage, platform; a projecting sheif (in a wall).
s.f. An ornament worn on the upper arm, a kind of armlet.
s.m. (local) = ṭānḍā, q.v.
s.m. A train or line (of cattle, &c.); a caravan (of merchants); the goods of a banjārā, q.v.; a venture (of goods); baggage, equipage; household furniture
subs: v.n. Baggage, &c. to be loaded or packed; to set out (on a journey); to depart (this life), to die.

def: s.m. commonly ṭāṅḍā, q.v.
s.m. The commander or head of a ṭānḍā or body of men (as workmen, lascars, labourers, &c.).
s.m. Dancing (esp. with violent gesticulation); the frantic dance of the god Śiva and his votaries.
s.m. See ṭaṇḍel.
s.m. corr. of tirendā, q.v. s.m. The float of a fishing line; a float; a buoy ( = taret).

s.f. = ṭāṅḍ, q.v. s.f. (m.?), A scaffolding (in a field of corn, &c. on which a person keeps watch when the grain is ripe to frighten away birds and other animals), a raised seat, stage, platform; a projecting sheif (in a wall).
s.f. Lassitude, weariness, exhaustion, syncope; sleepiness, sluggishness.
adj. Tired, wearied, overcome with fatigue or sleep, sleepy, sluggish.
s.f. = tandrā, q.v. s.f. Lassitude, weariness, exhaustion, syncope; sleepiness, sluggishness.
s.m. Grain (especially rice) after threshing and winnowing.
s.m.f. A plant the seeds of which are used as a vermifuge.
s.m. corr. of tannūr, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تُنْدُور (tundūr).

def: adj. See s.v. tan.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تندرست (tandurust). By surface analysis, تَن (tan, “body”) + دُرُسْت (durust, “good, healthy”).

ety: Pehl. tang; Zend tangista, rt. tać = S. तञ्च्
def: adj. Contracted, straitened, confined, strait, narrow, tight; wanting, scarce, scanty, stinted, barren; distressed, poor, badly off; distracted, troubled, vexed; dejected, sad, sick (at heart); — s.m. A horse-belt, girth; — a bag, sack; half a horse's, bullock's, or camel's load; — a bell
subs: or ba-tang ānā (-se), To be distressed or incommoded (by), to be in distress or difficulty; to be troubled, or vexed, or harassed (by); to be utterly weary or sick (of), to have one's patience exhausted (by); to be dejected or sad

adj. Straitened in circumstances, poor, distressed, destitute, in great straits
s.f. Narrow circumstances, straits, distress, poverty, adversity

s.f. Poverty, penury, want; inability (to do any thing), helplessness; — parsimony, niggardliness

s.f. Niggardliness, stinginess, parsimony; — heart-ache, grief, sorrow, dejection ( = dil-tangī)

v.n. To be in straits, be badly off, be penniless
or ba tang honā, v.n. = tang ānā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنگ (tang).

def: s.f. The leg (from the hip to the foot); a share ( = ṭāṅk); a fourth part, a quarter (in the language of brokers)
subs: v.n. 'To raise the leg'; to copulate
(-meṅ), To step in obtrusively, to interfere (in), intermeddle
s.m. 'Leg force or twist'; a trick in wrestling
(-kī), To pass under the legs (of); to funk, to fail; to forfeit; to submit, yield
(-ko), 'To give the leg' (to); to invite, or to yield to, the embraces (of a lover); to be a catamite
(-kī), To seize or lay hold of the leg (a dog, &c.)
v.n. The legs to give way or fail; to have gout in the legs.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀝𑀁𑀓 (ṭaṃka), from Sanskrit टङ्क (ṭaṅka). First attested as Middle Hindi ٿانگ (ṭang /⁠ṭang⁠/) / ٿنگ (ṭng /⁠ṭng⁠/).

def: s.m. Borax ( = ṭank).

s.f. A leg ( = ṭāṅg, q.v.). adj. Contracted, straitened, confined, strait, narrow, tight; wanting, scarce, scanty, stinted, barren; distressed, poor, badly off; distracted, troubled, vexed; dejected, sad, sick (at heart); — s.m. A horse-belt, girth; — a bag, sack; half a horse's, bullock's, or camel's load; — a bell

s.f. Narrow circumstances, straits, distress, poverty, adversity

v.n. To be in straits, be badly off, be penniless
or ba tang honā, v.n. = tang ānā, q.v.

def: s.m. A small two-wheeled car (without covering, and on which one person only can sit), a 'tonga'
subs: s.m. One who rides on a tāṅgā.

def: s.m. A denomination of money, equal to two paisās (see ṭakā).
adj. Having legs, legged (used chiefly in comp.); — s.m. A large reedy kind of grass, Saccharum procrum (it grows on high ground, and is usually as high as a man's legs, whence its name)
s.m. A hill pony.
v.t. To cause to be hung up; to hang up ( = ṭāngnā); — ṭaṅgā-denā, v.t. intens. of and = ṭaṅgānā.

ety: from ṭaṅgānā; — āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Hanging, suspension ( = laṭkāʼo).
s.f. A small axe.
s.f. = tangī, q.v. s.f. Straitness, narrowness, tightness, closeness; scantiness, scarcity, distress, difficulty, want, poverty; stinginess, parsimony; — a bag, sack
subs: v. To practise parsimony, to be parsimonious, to act the miser.

def: s.m. = ṭāṅgan, q.v. s.m. A hill pony.
s.f. A hatchet ( = ṭāṛī).
s.f. Straitness, narrowness, tightness, closeness; scantiness, scarcity, distress, difficulty, want, poverty; stinginess, parsimony; — a bag, sack
subs: v. To practise parsimony, to be parsimonious, to act the miser.

def: s.f. The plant Clypea hernandifolia.
v.t. To make (a horse) gallop.
v.t. To tighten (the girths of a horse, &c.).
v.t. To hang up by a string, to hang up, dangle, suspend
v.n. To hang, to be hung, to be suspended; to rear (a horse)
subs: v.n. To be hung up, &c. = ṭangnā.

def: s.f. See s.v. tang. adj. Contracted, straitened, confined, strait, narrow, tight; wanting, scarce, scanty, stinted, barren; distressed, poor, badly off; distracted, troubled, vexed; dejected, sad, sick (at heart); — s.m. A horse-belt, girth; — a bag, sack; half a horse's, bullock's, or camel's load; — a bell

adj. Straitened in circumstances, poor, distressed, destitute, in great straits
s.f. Narrow circumstances, straits, distress, poverty, adversity

v.n. To be in straits, be badly off, be penniless
or ba tang honā, v.n. = tang ānā, q.v.

def: s.f. prop. ṭangṛī, q.v.

ety: ṭaṅg, q.v.+rī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. Leg; thigh.

ety: ṭaṅg, q.v.+rī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. Leg; thigh.
v.t. = ṭangānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be hung up; to hang up ( = ṭāngnā); — ṭaṅgā-denā, v.t. intens. of and = ṭaṅgānā.
adj. & adv. Solitary, lonely; single; singular, unique; private; alone; only; apart, privately.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنها (tanhā), from Middle Persian tn'yhʾ (tanīhā).

def: s.f. Loneliness, solitude; privacy.
ex: میں بچ بھی جاؤں تو تنہائی مار ڈالے گی
ma͠i bac bhī jāõ to tanhāī mār ḍālegī
Even if I survive, loneliness will kill me
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنهایی (tanhāyī). By surface analysis, تنہا (tanhā) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. The warp ( = tānā, q.v.); — price paid for weaving
subs: s.m. The warp and the woof.

def: s.f. = tanayā, q.v. s.f. A daughter; a female descendant
s.f. The string or fasteniry of a garment (as of an angarkhā, &c.).
s.f. Bestowing abundance, putting (one) in a state of comfort or ease; — saying naʻam, 'Yes' (in confirmation of an assertion, a debt, &c.), acknowledgement (of).

ety: inf. n. of طنّ 'to make a sound of a continued or reiterated kind'
def: s.f. A humming, buzzing; hum, buzz; ringing, tinkling, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of انث 'to be feminine'
def: s.f. The feminine gender.
s.m. A kind of covering for the wrist; a narrow strip of cloth for the loins, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of نجم 'to appear or rise' (a star)
def: s.f. Prognosticating or calculating (an event) by the aspect of the stars; science of the stars, astrology.
s.m. A weight of four māshās; — s.f. An iron pin; a rivet; a stitch; part, portion, share; the parts of a bow; — assessment; appraisement, valuation.

s.m. A weight equal to four māshās; a sword, scimitar; a sacrificial knife; a hatchet; a stone-cutter's chisel; a spade, hoe; borax; a slope; a kind of elephant-apple or wood-apple; a weight of silver put for a coin
s.m. A pin, a rivet; a stitch; a join; solder; a needle-full of thread (or as much as would be used in one sewing); — a tub made of stones
subs: ṭāṅkā phernā (-meṅ), To put a stitch (in), pass a stitch (through)
ṭāṅkā lagānā (-meṅ), To put a stitch, or a rivet, &c. (in); see ṭāṅke lagānā
(-ke), Stitches to come open or undone; rivets or fastenings to become loosened; (fig.) to be unhinged, be undone, ruined, or destroyed
(-ke), Stitches to open out or be undone; (fig.) to have one's secrets, or designs, &c. exposed or betrayed
(-meṅ), To put some stitches (in), to stitch (a garment, or a wound, &c.); to rivet; to solder; to join.

def: s.m. Jingle, clink, ring (of plates, dishes, &c. = ṭanak, q.v.). s.f. A harsh sound; jingle, tinkle, ring (of metal, china, &c.); throb, beat, shooting pain; spirit, mettle.
s.f. = ṭāṅk, q.v. s.m. A weight of four māshās; — s.f. An iron pin; a rivet; a stitch; part, portion, share; the parts of a bow; — assessment; appraisement, valuation.
s.m. The mulberry, Morus indica.
s.f. = ṭaṅk, q.v. s.m. A weight of four māshās; — s.f. An iron pin; a rivet; a stitch; part, portion, share; the parts of a bow; — assessment; appraisement, valuation.
s.m. A blackguard, lecher, libertine.
s.f. The twang (of a bow-string); sound; howling; surprise, wonder; fame, notoriety.
s.m. incorr. for tangār, q.v.
v.n. To sound; to strike (as a clock or gong); to shudder (?).
s.m. An underground reservoir for water, a tank.
s.f. Wages, pay, salary, allowance, an assignment (on the revenue), an order for wages, &c.
subs: s.m. One who receives pay or salary; a holder of an order for wages, &c.
s.f. Receiving pay or a salary; service
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنخواه (tanxwāh).

def: s.f. A stone-cutter's chisel, a chisel; a tooth (as of a saw), a notch, jag; a dent, hollow, small hole, pit; a small piece cut or scooped out (as of a melon, &c. to examine the quality); a venereal chancre or shanker
subs: (-kī), 'Builder's chisel to sound or to be busy,' a building or structure to be in course of erection.

ety: inf. n. ii of نقح 'to extract' (marrow), &c.
def: s.f. Cleaning, polishing; clearing (the bowels), purging; — deciding (disputes), investigation, examination, sifting, search, inquiry, ascertaining
subs: adj. Demanding inquiry or investigation; to be decided or determined; for decision
(-kī), To decide, determine; to ascertain; to sift, go to the bottom (of).

ety: ṭāṅk˚ = Prk. टक्क(इ) or टक्के(इ) = S. टङ्कय(ति) or टङ्क(ति), rt. टङ्क
def: v.t. To join, fasten, tie; to stitch; to cobble; to rivet; to solder; to cement; to tack (on or to), to append attach, annex; to make a note or memorandum of; to record, enter, pen; — to swallow, gulp down, eat up; — ṭāṅk-jānā, Idem

ety: ṭaṅk˚ = Prk. टंक(इ); S. टङ्क (ति), rt. टङ्क
def: v.n. To be stitched ( = ṭaknā, q.v.). v.t. To look at, view, gaze on, behold; to stare at; to watch for; — v.n. To peep, spy, watch; to aim (at, -par).
s.f. Twang, sound (of a bow)
v.n. To twang.

ety: for A. تنقية tanqiyat, inf. n. ii of نقي 'to extract marrow' (from)
def: s.m. Cleaning; clearing out (the bowels), purging; winnowing (grain); — deciding (cases); decision (cf. tanqīḥ).
adj. & s.m. That alone, that merely, that itself.

ety: tān˚ = Prk. ताण(इ), or ताणे(इ) = S. तानय(ति), caus. of rt. तन्
def: v.t. To extend, stretch, expand; to pull, pull over (as a sheet, &c.); to tighten; to contract; knit (the brow); to pitch (a tent); to string up, to hang; to send in succession (as letters, &c.); to bring before (a magistrate), to sue; — v.n. To lie full length; to sleep; to die
v.n. See تننا tannā.
v.t. See ٿاننا ṭānnā.
v.t. To pull, &c. ( = tānnā, the proper form).

v.n. = tannā, q.v. v.t. To extend, stretch, expand; to pull, pull over (as a sheet, &c.); to tighten; to contract; knit (the brow); to pitch (a tent); to string up, to hang; to send in succession (as letters, &c.); to bring before (a magistrate), to sue; — v.n. To lie full length; to sleep; to die

v.n. To stretch, be pulled tight; to be spun out; to spread; to be pitched (a tent); to sit upright or erect; to move or act in a pompous or conceited manner, to make a display; — v.t. To pull tight, &c. = tānnā, q.v.
v.n. 'To be stretched;' to be straight, to be erect; — to twang, sound (as the string of an instrument); to have a sharp pricking pain; to tingle ( = tantanānā).
adj. & s.m. Facetious, jocose, jocund, ludicrous, playful, mirthful; — one who ridicules, a derider, mocker, scoffer.

ety: t̤annāz+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Playfulness, jocoseness, facetiousness; raillery, ridicule.
s.m. See s.v. tat. adj Heated, warmed; hot, warm; heating (cf. tattā).

s.f. (prov.) = ṭāṅḍ, and ṭāṅḍī, qq.v. s.f. (m.?), A scaffolding (in a field of corn, &c. on which a person keeps watch when the grain is ripe to frighten away birds and other animals), a raised seat, stage, platform; a projecting sheif (in a wall).
s.m. Dancing (esp. with violent gesticulation); the frantic dance of the god Śiva and his votaries.

s.f. (prov.) = ṭāṅḍ, and ṭāṅḍī, qq.v. s.f. (m.?), A scaffolding (in a field of corn, &c. on which a person keeps watch when the grain is ripe to frighten away birds and other animals), a raised seat, stage, platform; a projecting sheif (in a wall).

ety: inf. n. ii of نصف 'to reach to the middle' (of)
def: s.f. Dividing in the middle, or into two equal parts; bisecting; bisection

ety: inf. n. ii of نسخ 'to cancel,' &c.
def: s.f. Causing to annul or abrogate; cancelling; abrogation, quashing (an indictment or a decision).

ety: tāṅs˚ = S. त्रासय(ति), caus. or rt. त्रस्
def: v.t. To terrify, alarm, frighten; to threaten, menace; to treat with severity.
s.f. Thread, fibre, &c.; catgut, sinew; wire; string of a musical instrument; — a loom
subs: lit. 'The string sounded and he knew the tune'; he understood from the first word

def: adj. & adv. Precise, exact; opportune; — precisely, exactly, just; opportunely; — s.m. Precise or exact moment, opportune moment, nick of time ( = tat samay), critical moment; need, occasion.
s.f. Thread, string, wire, chord of a musical instrument ( = tāṅt, of which it may be a contraction)
s.m. Chief or essential part, essence, main point ( = tattwa); principal doctrine; model, typical form.
s.f. The crown of the head; (contemptuously) the pate, skull.
s.m. Series, row, range, line, string, train; race, posterity, generation.
s.m. See ṭanṭā.
s.m. Wrangling, altercation, noisy squabble or quarrel, brawl, row
subs: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome; — a quarrelsome person, a brawler.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Well-knit, compact, stout, strong, powerful, sturdy; firm, solid; stiff, hard.
adj. = tanṭāl, q.v.
s.m. Quarrel, &c. = ṭanṭā, q.v.
subs: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome; — a quarrelsome person ( = ṭanṭe-bāz).

def: s.m. Sound, noise, cry, shout, din, uproar; rumour, fame; pomp, dignity, state.
s.m. = tantanā, q.v. s.m. Sound, noise, cry, shout, din, uproar; rumour, fame; pomp, dignity, state.

ety: for A. طنطنة, rt. طنّ; see t̤anīn
def: s.m. Sound, din, clamour, hubbub, noise; fame; rumour; pomp, dignity, state; pride, vanity; imperiousness, domineering.

ety: fr. tantanānā; — ˚āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+त्वं
def: s.f. Tingling, smart; sharp pricking pain (arising from a burn or from inflammation).

ety: tantanā˚ = S. तन् 'to resound,' redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि
def: v.n. To sound, ring, to twang; to tingle.
v.t. To sound or ring (a bell, &c.); to tune (an instrument).
adj. (f. -ī), Well-knit, compact, stout, strong, powerful, sturdy; firm, solid; stiff, hard.

ety: ṭāṅṭh ā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Compactness, closeness, stoutness, sturdiness, solidity, firmness, stiffness, hardness.
s.f. = tāntā, q.v. s.m. Series, row, range, line, string, train; race, posterity, generation.
s.m. A weaver.
s.m. A weaver.
adj. & s.m. Having wires or strings (as a musical instrument); — the music of a stringed instrument.

s.m. Leading or principal action of a ceremony, the regular order of ceremonies or rites, ritual; chief or essential part, main point; principal doctrine, rule, theory; a scientific work; a religious treatise teaching magical and mystic formularies for the worship of the deities or the attainment of superhuman power; a medicament, a drug, a chief remedy or charm considered as producing medicinal effects; charm, spell, incantation, enchantment; — demonstration, clear and right conclusion; the right way of doing anything; — raiment, vesture.
s.m. A musician; — one who follows a tantra; a practiser of enchantments.
adj. & s.m. Relating to the Tāntras; taught or contained in the Tāntras; following the Tāntras; — one who is completely versed in any science or system; a scholar; a philosopher.
s.m. Thread, cord, wire, string, line; warp of a web; cobweb; filament; a line of descendants; offspring, issue, race
s.m. = tāʼū, q.v. s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.
adj. Thin, slender, delicate, &c. ( = tanuk); — s.f. The body, &c. ( = tan, q.v.)
subs: s.m. A pore of the skin
adj. Having slender limbs, delicately formed, delicate, slim.

def: adj. & adv. Little, small, slight, thin; very little, the least; — a little, slightly; a little while
adj. Robust, corpulent.
wety: From Classical Persian تنومند (tanūmand).

ety: Pehl. tanūr; Zend tanūra: prob a Semitic word, connected with nār
def: s.m. An oven; a stove.
s.m. Darkness before the eyes, fainting, a swoon.
s.m. Copper ( = tāṅbā, q.v.). s.m. Copper
subs: s.m. A copper rupee, a bad rupee
s.m. Sulphate of copper, blue vitriol.

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts ( = tuwar).
s.f. A delicate or slender woman; — tanwī, s.f. Name of a plant; — (in Pros.) a kind of stanza of four lines with twenty-four syllables in each.

ety: inf. n. ii of نون not in use; formed from nūn, 'the letter ṉ'
def: s.f. Nunation, doubling the short vowels at the end of (Arabic) words in writing, and pronouncing them with the addition of the sound ṉ (e.g. خليفةً ḵẖalīfataṉ; تخميناً taḵẖmīnaṉ: see Grammar).
adv. See s.v. tanu. s.m. = tāʼū, q.v. s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.
s.f. A delicate or slender woman; — tanwī, s.f. Name of a plant; — (in Pros.) a kind of stanza of four lines with twenty-four syllables in each.

ety: inf. n. ii of نور 'to shine'
def: s.f. Illuminating, enlightening; illumination.
s.m. Mirth, joking, pleasantry; laughing (at), mocking, ridiculing; ridicule, derision, sneer, scoff, taunt, sarcasm
subs: (-kī), To mock, jeer, laugh (at), ridicule, deride, &c.

def: adv. In joke; jeeringly, tauntingly, sneeringly, ironically, &c.
s.f. See s.v. tan.
s.f. Keeping apart from all impurity; purifying, cleansing; purity, holiness.

ety: inf. n. ii of نزل 'to descend'
def: s.f. Causing (a traveller, &c.) to alight, receiving (one) hospitably; revelation; the Qorʼān.
